task_completion.description: The agent created and saved the experiment as 'shape_task.psyexp'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The PsychoPy Builder window is open, and the window title or the main experiment settings show the name 'shape_task' or 'shape_task.psyexp'.

task_completion.description: The agent added a loop to the Flow and loaded the legacy conditions CSV file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A loop (e.g., 'trials') is visible in the Flow panel at the bottom. The loop properties dialog shows 'legacy_conditions.csv' in the 'Conditions' field.

task_completion.description: The agent added Image and Text components to display the shape stimulus and the 'match_text'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A routine (e.g., 'trial') contains an Image component (for the shape) and a Text component. The Text component's text field should be set to a variable like '$match_text'.

task_completion.description: The agent added a Keyboard component to collect 'y' and 'n' responses.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A Keyboard component is present in the trial routine. Its properties dialog shows 'Allowed keys' set to include 'y' and 'n' (e.g., 'y','n').

task_completion.description: The agent implemented logic to handle the Windows-style absolute paths from the legacy CSV.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A Code component is visible in the trial routine containing Python code to manipulate the path string (e.g., using .split('\\') or .replace()), OR the Image component's 'Image' field contains an inline Python expression to fix the path.

task_completion.description: The agent implemented a feedback routine that provides color-coded text ('Correct!' in green, 'Wrong!' in red).
task_completion.visual_evidence: A second routine (e.g., 'feedback') is inside the loop in the Flow panel. It contains a Text component and a Code component with if/else logic checking the keyboard response's '.corr' attribute to set the text to 'Correct!'/'Wrong!' and the color to green/red.

integrity.description: The agent used the PsychoPy Builder GUI to construct the experiment.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent interacting with the PsychoPy Builder interface (adding components, editing properties dialogs) rather than just writing XML or Python code in a text editor.

integrity.description: The agent used variables for stimuli and text rather than hardcoding the trials.
integrity.visual_evidence: Component properties use variables starting with '$' (e.g., $match_text, $image_path) instead of hardcoded strings for the shapes and text.

integrity.description: The agent saved the file to the correct directory using the application's save dialog.
integrity.visual_evidence: A 'Save As' dialog or file explorer is visible at some point, saving the file to '/home/ga/PsychoPyExperiments/'.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The experiment must be saved to '/home/ga/PsychoPyExperiments/shape_task.psyexp'. The conditions file is located at '/home/ga/PsychoPyExperiments/legacy_conditions.csv' and contains Windows-style absolute paths that need to be adapted to load stimuli from '/home/ga/PsychoPyExperiments/stimuli'. The stimuli are circle, square, and triangle images.
